product_overview: TG Clinic Management System is a React-based clinic web application for managing core administrative workflows including authentication, customers, employees, appointments, payments, services, reports, and configuration. It supports role-based access, consultant (CTV) management, and cosmetic line-of-business operations.
core_goals: Provide secure admin authentication and role-based access control
Enable efficient management of customers, employees, and consultants
Support appointment scheduling and status tracking across calendar views
Process payments, allocations, and payment history review
Manage service catalogs, permissions, reports, and clinic settings
Support cosmetic business workflows and consultant hierarchy management

features.name: Authentication and Authorization
features.description: Allows clinic administrators to log in securely and access the application based on assigned roles and permissions.
user_flows: POST /login with valid email/password credentials -> Receive 200 and authenticated session -> GET /overview with session -> Receive overview dashboard data
POST /login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 unauthorized response -> Remain on /login with error message

features.name: Customer Management
features.description: Supports customer search, profile viewing, creation, editing, and deletion with confirmation, including accent-insensitive search.
user_flows: GET /customers -> Receive 200 with customer list -> Search customers using accent-insensitive query -> Receive filtered results -> GET /customers/{id} -> Receive 200 with customer profile -> POST /customers with valid form data -> Receive 201 created -> PUT /customers/{id} with updated data -> Receive 200 updated customer
GET /customers -> Receive 200 with customer list -> DELETE /customers/{id} without confirmation -> Receive 400 or blocked action -> Customer remains unchanged

features.name: Employee Management with Permission Groups
features.description: Provides employee CRUD operations, search, and assignment of permission groups for access control.
user_flows: GET /employees -> Receive 200 with employee list -> Search employees by name or keyword -> Receive filtered results -> POST /employees with valid employee data and permission group -> Receive 201 created -> PUT /employees/{id} to update employee data or group -> Receive 200 updated employee
DELETE /employees/{id} without sufficient permission -> Receive 403 forbidden -> Employee record is not deleted

features.name: Appointment Calendar
features.description: Enables scheduling and management of appointments across day, week, and month calendar views with doctor/CTV assignment and status updates.
user_flows: GET /calendar?view=day -> Receive 200 with day appointments -> POST /appointments with valid customer, time, and assignee -> Receive 201 created -> PUT /appointments/{id} to change status -> Receive 200 updated appointment
GET /calendar?view=month -> Receive 200 with month appointments -> PUT /appointments/{id} with invalid time or missing required fields -> Receive 400 validation error -> Appointment remains unchanged

features.name: Payment Processing
features.description: Supports payment history review, payment allocation, and VietQR-based payment workflows.
user_flows: GET /payments -> Receive 200 with payment history -> POST /payments/allocation with valid allocation data -> Receive 200 allocation confirmed -> Payment history reflects updated allocation
POST /payments/allocation with invalid amount or missing invoice reference -> Receive 400 validation error -> Allocation is rejected

features.name: Service Catalog Management
features.description: Allows administrators to manage services and categories in the clinic service catalog.
user_flows: GET /services -> Receive 200 with service list -> POST /services with valid service data -> Receive 201 created -> PUT /services/{id} with updated pricing or category -> Receive 200 updated service
GET /services/{id} with non-existent service id -> Receive 404 not found -> No catalog changes applied

features.name: Permission Matrix and Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Provides permission group and matrix management for assigning feature-level access to employees and roles.
user_flows: GET /settings/permissions -> Receive 200 with permission matrix -> PUT /settings/permissions with updated role permissions -> Receive 200 saved permissions -> Employees inherit updated access rules
PUT /settings/permissions with unauthorized user or invalid permission payload -> Receive 403 forbidden or 400 validation error -> Permission matrix remains unchanged

features.name: Reports and Excel Export
features.description: Lets users view operational reports and export them to Excel for offline analysis.
user_flows: GET /reports -> Receive 200 with report data -> POST /reports/export with Excel export request -> Receive 200 with downloadable file link or binary export
POST /reports/export with unsupported format or missing filters -> Receive 400 validation error -> Export is not generated

features.name: CTV Module with Hierarchy
features.description: Supports consultant (CTV) management, hierarchy tracking, and commission monitoring.
user_flows: GET /ctv -> Receive 200 with consultant list -> POST /ctv with valid consultant data -> Receive 201 created -> GET /ctv/{id}/hierarchy -> Receive 200 with hierarchy details -> GET /commission -> Receive 200 with commission tracking data
POST /ctv with duplicate or invalid consultant data -> Receive 400 validation error -> Consultant is not created

features.name: Cosmetic Line of Business Support
features.description: Enables switching between business lines and managing cosmetic services and cosmetic clients.
user_flows: POST /settings/lob/switch with cosmetic line of business selected -> Receive 200 and active LOB updated -> GET /customers?lob=cosmetic -> Receive 200 with cosmetic client list -> GET /services?lob=cosmetic -> Receive 200 with cosmetic services
POST /settings/lob/switch with unsupported business unit -> Receive 400 validation error -> Active line of business remains unchanged

features.name: CTV Self-Service Portal
features.description: Consultant (CTV) facing portal where a logged-in consultant views their referral dashboard, earnings/commission journey stages, refers customers, and manages their own profile. Separate from the admin app surface.
user_flows: Login as a CTV account -> Land on CTV dashboard -> View referral list and earnings journey stages (born from payments) -> Open refer modal -> Select service and add notes -> Submit referral -> Referral persists and appears in dashboard
Open CTV profile (Me tab) -> Edit own profile fields -> Save -> Receive 200 and updated profile -> Change password via ChangePassword modal -> Receive confirmation
Attempt to access an admin-only route as CTV -> Route is gated / redirected -> No admin data exposed

features.name: Public Guest Appointment Booking (Phone-First)
features.description: Unauthenticated public booking flow reachable via a CTV referral link. Guest verifies by phone, the form auto-populates known customer data, and an appointment is created and attributed to the referring CTV.
user_flows: Open public CTV booking link (/ctv/join or public booking page) -> Enter phone number -> Receive verification -> Known customer fields auto-populate -> Choose service and preferred time -> Submit -> Appointment created and attributed to referring CTV
Submit booking with missing required fields or invalid phone -> Receive validation error -> Appointment is not created -> Guest remains on the form with guidance

features.name: Commission Tracking (Per-Service, Multi-Level)
features.description: Commission computed from sale order CTV attribution and per-level commission config against paid amounts, with payout state tracking and delete/refund guards.
user_flows: GET /commission -> Receive 200 with commission rows derived from paid sale orders and configured levels -> Drill into a CTV -> View per-service commission breakdown and payout status
Attempt to delete a payment that has already paid-out commission -> Receive 409 conflict guard -> Payment and commission remain unchanged
